name: Soap Making
tagline: Turning fat and strong alkali into surfactant that removes pathogens from skin and fabric — hygiene without petrochemical supply chains
problem:
Skin and cloth carry invisible films of fat, protein, and dirt that shelter bacteria and viruses from mechanical removal by water alone. Historical mortality curves bend when cities adopt routine handwashing and laundry — not because soap kills every germ instantly, but because it emulsifies oils so water can rinse pathogens away. In collapse, when antibiotics are absent or counterfeit, preventing wound contamination and diarrhoeal spread through clean hands is higher leverage than almost any single drug.

The manufacturing problem is chemical danger: strong alkali from wood leachate or commercial lye can blind on splash and dissolve skin on contact. Saponification is exothermic; poorly mixed batches trap caustic pockets inside bars that only reveal themselves in a wash basin. Cold-process soap also requires patience — bars must cure until pH drops to non-irritating ranges for skin.

Economically, soap ties to fat supply: every society that preserves animals has tallow; plant-oil soaps are possible where oil palms, olives, or other lipid crops exist. Without fat chemistry literacy, people burn lean meat for calories and throw away the rendering stream that could have become hygiene infrastructure.

Professional verification is required before use on a human being for any medicinal claim beyond general hygiene; contraindications include known contact allergy to fragrance oils or botanicals; overdose risk applies to ingesting lye or raw soap; sterility is not surgical-grade from kitchen batches — do not confuse household soap with sterile field surgery supplies.
overview:
Soap is the salt of a fatty acid: triglycerides hydrolyse under hot alkali into glycerol and long-chain carboxylates that have a water-loving head and a grease-loving tail. In water, micelles form that lift oils off surfaces so rinse water carries them away.

Traditional soft soap begins by leaching hardwood ash with hot water to produce potassium hydroxide solution of unknown strength — titration is historical craft; modern equivalents use measured sodium hydroxide for repeatability. Fat is rendered by gentle heating with minimal burning; strained tallow is combined with lye at calculated superfat percentages so finished bars retain a margin of unsaponified oil for mildness.

Cold process mixes oils and lye near trace (thickening) then moulds; hot process finishes saponification in a pot before moulding, shortening cure for field use at some cost in texture control. Laundry soap can be softer and more alkaline than skin soap if kept colour-coded and stored apart.

Hard water consumes soap by forming insoluble scum with calcium and magnesium; washing soda or chelating rinse strategies help in marginal water. On Mars, imported surfactants dominate early phases; local synthesis from regolith-derived chemicals is research-grade, not cottage industry yet.

principles.name: Saponification stoichiometry
principles.explanation: Each oil has a saponification value — grams of KOH to fully react; superfat leaves unreacted oil for mildness.

principles.name: pH and skin barrier
principles.explanation: Fresh soap can be harsh; cure time allows equilibration; patch-test before communal distribution.

principles.name: Micelle action
principles.explanation: Surfactants lower surface tension so water wets skin and carries away emulsified grime.

principles.name: Caustic hazard
principles.explanation: Alkali solutions are weight-dense and splash invisibly — engineering controls first.

components.name: Ash leaching vessel
components.madeFrom: Perforated barrel, hardwood ash, boiled water
components.criticalNote: Strength varies by ash tree species

components.name: Mixing pot and stirrer
components.function: Combines fat and lye with controlled heat
components.criticalNote: Never use aluminium with hot lye

components.name: Wire cutter or knife
components.criticalNote: Wear gloves — centre may still be caustic

components.name: Curing racks with airflow
components.function: Drives excess water and completes slow reactions
components.criticalNote: Label date; track pH if strips available

rawMaterials.name: Hardwood ash
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Imported lye tablets until closed-loop plant ash or electrolytic alkali exists
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Sift charcoal chunks; leach with measured hot water volumes; filter

rawMaterials.name: Animal or plant fat
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Recovered lipids from food waste streams in habitats; synthetic fatty acids only at advanced chemical plants
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Render low and slow; filter cracklings

rawMaterials.name: Water
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Recycled greywater never for lye — use potable loop only
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Boil and cool if biological contamination suspected

buildSteps.title: Calculate lye strength before touching fat
buildSteps.description: If using unknown ash leach, float an egg or potato until approximate buoyancy matches published traditional tests — record everything. Prefer weighed commercial lye with SDS if available.
buildSteps.warningNote: Lye solution causes permanent eye damage on splash — full face shield and vinegar rinse nearby never neutralise safely on skin without copious water after

buildSteps.title: Render fat completely clear
buildSteps.description: Strain twice through cloth; re-melt and settle salts to bottom; decant only oil phase.
buildSteps.warningNote: Hot fat burns deeper than boiling water — long sleeves and no water into oil

buildSteps.title: Mix lye into water never reverse
buildSteps.description: Always add solid lye slowly to water while stirring; heat rises; work under ventilation.
buildSteps.warningNote: Adding water to solid lye can eject boiling caustic spray

buildSteps.title: Bring oils and lye to trace and mould
buildSteps.description: Match temperatures per recipe; stir until pudding consistency; pour moulds on level surface.
buildSteps.warningNote: Exotherm can crack glass vessels — metal preferred

buildSteps.title: Cure, pH test, and label
buildSteps.description: Wait weeks; zap-strip pH if available; label batch and date; segregate laundry soap visually from skin soap.
buildSteps.warningNote: Caustic pockets in under-stirred soap — discard suspicious layers

history.event: Sumerian tablets describe alkali washing agents.
Leblanc and later Solvay processes industrialise alkali.
Synthetic detergents supplement but do not erase soap chemistry.

impact:
Soap is population medicine disguised as household chore. It reduces diarrhoea and surgical sepsis when used with clean water.

Space habitats will still need surfactants compatible with water recycling membranes.

warnings: Contraindications: known severe contact allergy to specific oils or fragrances used in batch; discontinue if rash or respiratory irritation occurs.
Overdose risk: ingestion of lye or raw soap causes corrosive burns to mouth, oesophagus, and stomach — medical emergency; keep lye locked away from children.
Sterility: kitchen soap is not sterile surgical scrub; do not use it as a substitute for sterile field supplies in deep contaminated wounds.
Professional verification is required before use on a human being. This is especially important for any claim beyond routine hygiene and wound cleaning in non-sterile environments.

sources.title: WHO — Hand hygiene in health care
